purpose: Reusable definition-of-done templates for implementation agents. Templates preserve each workstream's ambition while making required evidence, tests, logs, freshness checks, claim gates, and closure commands explicit.

templates.summary: Keep semantic fallback, no-op, unsupported, bootstrap, and abort-only contracts visible after support-matrix joins.
start_conditions: br --no-db show <bead-id> --json returns the target semantic-overlay bead
Semantic inventory and symbol-join artifacts parse as JSON
Adjacent semantic-overlay beads are not already in_progress
blocked_by_checks: Do not edit a row owned by another in_progress semantic-overlay bead
Block closure when support_matrix or version-script joins are stale
Block README or FEATURE_PARITY claim changes without matching semantic evidence
required_unit_test_classes: claim promotion blocked by semantic divergence
artifact_freshness_rules: Regenerate reports under target/conformance before closure
Every semantic row must cite source_path and artifact_refs
Fresh source_commit in emitted logs must match git rev-parse HEAD
user_facing_claim_gates: README and FEATURE_PARITY may not count support taxonomy as semantic parity
Compatibility reports must expose fallback/no-op limitations
Full parity language is blocked until all joined rows are semantically full
closure_commands: rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test semantic_contract_inventory_test
rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test semantic_contract_symbol_join_test
known_limitations_policy: Record unsupported, compat_noop, deterministic_fallback, limited_bootstrap, and abort_only rows explicitly
Open follow-up beads for newly discovered untracked contracts
non_goals: Do not implement missing libc behavior while only validating the overlay
Do not delete temporary contracts from artifacts to make a gate pass

templates.summary: Separate tracker/tooling failures from code or parity failures so agents can safely continue implementation work.
start_conditions: br --no-db list --status in_progress,claimed --json or direct JSONL inspection identifies live owners
Open/in_progress count is captured before edits
Current failure signature is recorded with exact command and exit status
blocked_by_checks: Do not run DB-backed br commands when the dispatch says DB is corrupt
Do not run br sync --flush-only when stale exports are known
Do not overwrite peer agent JSONL edits
artifact_freshness_rules: Report must include command, exit_status, duration_ms, and tracker_state for every probe
Report must identify implementation work that may safely continue despite tool failure
Generated timestamps must be newer than the latest tracker edit used as input
user_facing_claim_gates: Dashboards must not report zero-open if JSONL still has open parity work
Tracker repair closure cannot claim code quality improvement without code validation evidence
closure_commands: rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test tracker_health_report_test
known_limitations_policy: List stale commands and whether they block implementation
Preserve recovery artifacts and record why DB-backed commands were skipped
non_goals: Do not silently rebuild or delete tracker databases
Do not close parity beads based only on tracker health

templates.summary: Convert feature-ledger gaps into executable evidence and prevent prose claims from outrunning artifacts.
start_conditions: Feature parity ledger and reality-check backlog parse
Target owner family is scoped to one closable gap or closure group
Existing evidence artifacts for the family are identified
blocked_by_checks: Block closure when ledger row count or owner-family mapping is stale
Block docs/release claim advancement without current evidence
Do not collapse distinct parity gaps into one vague closure
required_e2e_fixture_harness_scripts: rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test feature_parity_drift_gate_test
artifact_freshness_rules: Every closed gap cites current artifact_refs and source_commit
Missing proof/fixture rows remain visible as blockers
Generated reports fail when source artifacts are older than their inputs
user_facing_claim_gates: README, release notes, and FEATURE_PARITY cannot advance a family with unresolved ledger gaps
Owner-family summaries must include residual gaps and limitations
closure_commands: rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test feature_parity_drift_gate_test
known_limitations_policy: Open targeted follow-ups for any unclosed gap row
Record whether missing evidence is proof, fixture, user workload, or release-gate evidence
non_goals: Do not mark a ledger row done by moving it out of the source artifact
Do not broaden the bead past the selected owner family

templates.summary: Turn fixture families into executable conformance coverage with direct and isolated-subprocess paths.
start_conditions: Fixture family and symbol list are selected
Host-glibc oracle or golden artifact is available
Direct-vs-subprocess execution requirement is understood
blocked_by_checks: Block closure if fixture rows are only captured but never executed
Block closure if isolated-subprocess path is missing for crash/abort-prone cases
Do not hide skipped cases as passes
artifact_freshness_rules: Fixture manifest hash must match executed input
Subprocess transcripts must include exit_code, stderr summary, and failure_signature
Strict and hardened mode rows must be separate when behavior differs
user_facing_claim_gates: Fixture coverage can support conformance claims only for executed cases
Skipped optional host tools must remain visible in reports
known_limitations_policy: List unexecuted symbols and reasons
Open follow-ups for fixture families needing oracle expansion
non_goals: Do not rewrite fixture format without a migration bead
Do not claim broad symbol-family parity from one narrow fixture

templates.summary: Advance difficult libc surfaces with user-visible workload evidence instead of papering over semantic complexity.
start_conditions: Selected hard-part surface maps to a real workload or standards contract
Known glibc divergence is documented
Target crate and ABI module are scoped
blocked_by_checks: Block closure when only unit tests exist for externally visible process behavior
Block unsafe code without SAFETY comments at ABI boundaries
Do not replace complex semantics with compatibility shims
artifact_freshness_rules: Conformance artifact must cite mode, symbol, oracle_kind, expected, actual, and errno
Smoke summaries must be regenerated after ABI changes
Known divergences must remain linked to beads
user_facing_claim_gates: User-workload support claims require successful preload or integration evidence
Documented limitations must appear in compatibility reports
known_limitations_policy: Record glibc divergences with artifact_refs
Open follow-ups for workload failures outside the bead scope
non_goals: Do not line-by-line translate glibc
Do not broaden into unrelated hard surfaces

templates.summary: Guard L0/L1/L2/L3 replacement claims with proof matrices, call-through scans, and standalone evidence.
start_conditions: Target replacement level and affected claims are named
Replacement profile and call-through census parse
Current proof matrix row exists or a new row is added
blocked_by_checks: Block L1+ claims without replacement-level evidence
Block L2/L3 claims while forbidden host call-throughs remain
Block release tags when proof matrix rows are stale
artifact_freshness_rules: Call-through census must be regenerated after ABI source edits
Proof matrix source_commit must match HEAD for claim advancement
Replacement and interpose reports must stay separate
user_facing_claim_gates: Release notes cannot claim L1/L2/L3 without fresh proof rows
README examples must identify interpose-vs-standalone status
closure_commands: rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test standalone_readiness_matrix_test
known_limitations_policy: Record exact remaining forbidden call-through modules and symbols
Link L2/L3 blockers to follow-up beads
non_goals: Do not rename replacement levels
Do not mirror legacy branch names into docs

templates.summary: Keep fmt, check, clippy, test, UBS, and unrelated-failure state reproducible without hiding drift.
start_conditions: Changed files are listed
Affected crates are identified
Known unrelated validation drift is cited before new edits
blocked_by_checks: Block closure if changed files lack targeted validation
Block closure if new UBS warnings are untriaged
Do not normalize unrelated failures as pass
artifact_freshness_rules: Gate-health artifacts must include exact command, scope, exit status, and failure_signature
Targeted passing gates must be newer than the changed files
Workspace failures must name owning bead or explicit unknown owner
user_facing_claim_gates: Do not report green workspace validation if quarantined failures remain
Release claims require fresh changed-surface proof
known_limitations_policy: Record exact unrelated failures and owning beads
Open follow-up beads for unknown validation drift
non_goals: Do not reformat peer-owned files
Do not run broad workspace gates when a scoped crate gate is sufficient

templates.summary: Optimize only after capturing baselines, budgets, and host-glibc comparisons for the selected hot path.
start_conditions: Hot path and performance target are named
Baseline exists for current source_commit
Behavior proof or conformance fixture covers the optimized path
blocked_by_checks: Block optimization without a before/after benchmark
Block performance claims without correctness gates
Do not tune unrelated hot paths in the same bead
artifact_freshness_rules: Baseline and after reports must include source_commit and command
Benchmark target_dir must be isolated or recorded
Regression thresholds must fail closed
user_facing_claim_gates: Performance claims require host-glibc or prior-baseline comparison
Do not publish speedup without exact benchmark configuration
known_limitations_policy: Record noisy benchmarks and environment limits
Open follow-ups for unprofiled adjacent hot paths
non_goals: Do not optimize by weakening semantic checks
Do not claim global performance from one microbenchmark

templates.summary: Keep runtime-math and formal proof claims grounded in executable controller evidence and stale-source guards.
start_conditions: Runtime controller or proof binder surface is named
Manifest/report source path exists
Expected runtime decision law contribution is stated
blocked_by_checks: Block closure if proof artifacts are stale relative to source refs
Block runtime-math claims without live code or harness evidence
Do not substitute prose for executable guard output
required_e2e_fixture_harness_scripts: rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane runtime_math
artifact_freshness_rules: Manifest rows must cite source modules and fallback_when_data_missing
Proof traceability binder must fail stale source references
Runtime decision logs must include decision_path and replacement_level
user_facing_claim_gates: Formal/runtime-math claims require executable controller proof
Missing theorem machinery must be labeled as synthesis/proof-pipeline work
closure_commands: rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane runtime_math
known_limitations_policy: Record unavailable proof artifacts separately from runtime code failures
Open follow-ups for missing controller-manifest rows
non_goals: Do not add unused high-math prose without a gate
Do not change runtime policy semantics while only updating evidence

templates.summary: Tie compatibility and diagnostics claims to realistic workload bundles with visible fallback/mode decisions.
start_conditions: Target user persona or workload bundle is selected
Mode requirements are named
Expected diagnostics and semantic fallback surfaces are identified
blocked_by_checks: Block release examples when workload evidence is missing
Block compatibility reports that hide semantic fallback decisions
Do not report optional-tool skips as passes
required_e2e_fixture_harness_scripts: rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test real_program_smoke_suite_test
artifact_freshness_rules: Workload bundle must cite source_commit, mode, command, exit status, and semantic overlay rows
Compatibility report must be regenerated after workload or overlay changes
Fallback diagnostics must include symbol or api_family when available
user_facing_claim_gates: README examples require fresh workload evidence
Compatibility reports must distinguish pass, fail, skip, fallback, and unsupported
Release notes cannot promote workload support without current artifacts
closure_commands: rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test real_program_smoke_suite_test
known_limitations_policy: Record skipped optional tools with exact binary names
Open follow-up beads for failing realistic workloads
non_goals: Do not turn the README into a release claim without evidence
Do not add marketing-only workload prose

present_evidence: rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test semantic_contract_inventory_test

missing_evidence: rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test standalone_readiness_matrix_test

implementation_handoff_checklist.purpose: Pre-claim checklist for one-bead-at-a-time implementation agents. The checklist makes tracker state, dependency state, work surface, evidence, rch target policy, and next safe action explicit before an agent edits files.

pre_claim_checks: Read /dp/AGENTS.md, AGENTS.md, and README.md once
br --no-db ready --json includes the bead with status open
br --no-db show <bead-id> --json lists only closed blocking dependencies
br --no-db list --status in_progress --json does not show the bead
branches.next_safe_action: Claim with br --no-db update <bead-id> --status=in_progress --json, reserve exact files, then implement the smallest evidence-backed slice.
required_e2e: Run the bead-owned gate or harness script that emits target/conformance evidence
branches.source_commit_policy: Every emitted report or log row records git rev-parse HEAD.
branches.target_dir_policy: Use a unique CARGO_TARGET_DIR under /data/tmp or target/rch for rch cargo commands.
branches.commit_push_expectations: Set AGENT_NAME before commit and push main plus main:master when pushing.
branches.closure_notes: List exact commands, generated artifact paths, and whether unrelated peer changes were left untouched.

pre_claim_checks: DB-backed br or bv output disagrees with .beads/issues.jsonl
br --no-db list --status open --json still returns open work
br --no-db show <bead-id> --json identifies blockers or exact-ID split-brain
branches.next_safe_action: Use no-db JSONL state as source of truth; claim a ready unrelated bead or a tracker-repair bead, and record the stale command as tool failure rather than code failure.
required_e2e: br --no-db ready --json or br --no-db list --status open --json transcript
bv --robot-triage transcript when it returns without blocking
branches.source_commit_policy: Tracker reports record source_commit and command exit status for every probe.
branches.target_dir_policy: Do not retry failed rch builds in a full /tmp target; move target_dir to /data/tmp.
branches.commit_push_expectations: Commit only the claimed bead files and .beads export; preserve unrelated peer edits.
branches.closure_notes: Name stale commands, affected bead IDs, next safe action, and remaining tracker limitations.

pre_claim_checks: br ready --json is empty, hangs, or reports corrupted SQLite state
br --no-db list --status open --json returns candidate work
Direct .beads/issues.jsonl inspection agrees with the chosen candidate
branches.next_safe_action: Select from br --no-db list --status open --json, avoid in_progress rows, claim with br --no-db update, and keep closure honest if exact-ID mutation remains flaky.
required_e2e: No-db show transcript for dependency state
branches.source_commit_policy: Closure evidence cites the current source commit even when DB-backed br is skipped.
branches.target_dir_policy: Use a unique target dir per agent and bead.
branches.commit_push_expectations: Set AGENT_NAME and push only after no-db bead export is staged with code changes.
branches.closure_notes: Separate exact-ID tracker mutation failure from completed code/test evidence.

pre_claim_checks: git log or artifacts show the claimed dotted ID already shipped
br --no-db show <bead-id> --json still reports open or in_progress
Regression gates for the shipped surface can be rerun
branches.next_safe_action: Rerun the exact closure gate, update the bead with proof if mutation works, or create/claim the tracker-repair follow-up while reporting the already-shipped artifact paths.
required_e2e: Replay the shipped gate or harness command named by the old closure
branches.source_commit_policy: Report both shipped commit and current validation commit when they differ.
branches.target_dir_policy: Use a fresh target dir so old target artifacts do not masquerade as new proof.
branches.commit_push_expectations: Commit only tracker/evidence updates needed for the claimed closure.
branches.closure_notes: State whether the bead was code-complete before this turn and what proof was refreshed.

pre_claim_checks: git status lists files outside the intended work surface
Those files are not required for this bead
File reservations or direct inspection show no overlap
branches.next_safe_action: Leave unrelated files untouched, reserve the exact bead-owned files, and stage only the claimed files plus .beads changes.
required_e2e: Run only the bead-owned gate or targeted harness command
branches.source_commit_policy: Logs identify the source commit before staging.
branches.target_dir_policy: Use per-bead target dirs to avoid peer build contention.
branches.commit_push_expectations: Do not stash, revert, delete, or stage unrelated peer files.
branches.closure_notes: Mention that unrelated user or agent changes were not reverted.

pre_claim_checks: Targeted bead tests are green or have bead-owned failures
Repo-wide fmt, clippy, or test failures are known outside touched files
The bead acceptance allows scoped -p validation
branches.next_safe_action: Run scoped rch cargo gates for the touched crate, record unrelated workspace failures with exact diagnostics, and avoid widening the edit to unrelated debt.
required_e2e: Bead-owned gate script if externally visible behavior changed
branches.source_commit_policy: Known-failure rows cite the current source commit and command.
branches.target_dir_policy: Use a unique target dir and do not share target with another active agent.
branches.commit_push_expectations: Commit targeted proof and document residual unrelated failures.
branches.closure_notes: State which failures are bead-owned versus pre-existing workspace debt.

pre_claim_checks: br --no-db show <bead-id> --json lists open blockers
br --no-db ready --json excludes the bead
No force claim is needed for immediate code work
branches.next_safe_action: Do not claim the blocked bead; choose its ready blocker, create a narrow child if the blocker is too broad, or surface the exact blocker IDs.
required_tests: No code tests required unless a ready blocker is claimed
branches.source_commit_policy: Triage reports still record source_commit for reproducibility.
branches.target_dir_policy: No cargo target dir required until a code bead is claimed.
branches.commit_push_expectations: Only commit tracker changes when a child/follow-up bead is created.
branches.closure_notes: List blocker IDs and the concrete next safe bead.

observations: ready output includes bd-example-ready with status open
show output lists only closed blocking dependencies
in_progress output does not include bd-example-ready
bv robot diagnostics return no cycle or parent-blocker warning for the target
dry_run_transcripts.next_safe_action: Claim, reserve exact files, implement the smallest closure slice, then run scoped rch cargo gates.

observations: ready output is empty or stale
no-db open list still returns bd-example-stale
show output records the exact dependency state
bv disagreement is recorded as tracker/tool failure, not as a code blocker
required_e2e: Tracker report or bead-owned gate script
dry_run_transcripts.next_safe_action: Proceed from JSONL/no-db truth on the narrowest ready slice; keep moving past stale SQLite or stale bv output.

required_evidence: tool failure separated from code failure
missing_evidence: tool failure separated from code failure
